steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$f(4) = \frac{1}{2}(10) = 5.$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. It's given that the function $f$ is defined by $f\left(x\right)=\frac{1}{2}\left(x+6\right)$. Substituting $4$ for $x$ in the given function yields $f\left(4\right)=\frac{1}{2}\left(4+6\right)$, or $f\left(4\right)=5$. Therefore, the value of $f\left(4\right)$ is $5$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $2\left(4+6\right)$, not $\frac{1}{2}\left(4+6\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $2+\left(4+6\right)$, not $\frac{1}{2}\left(4+6\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $4+6$, not $\frac{1}{2}\left(4+6\right)$.
